summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Block’s Dorsey rethinks corporate structure
articles.description: As AI plays a bigger role in operations, the middle management layer becomes less important, the company’s head says.

articles.title: PayPal board lures ex-Square executive
articles.description: Under PayPal’s new CEO, the board has added a director who brings an industry-savvy perspective at a watershed moment for the company.

articles.title: Amazon ditches Amex for Mastercard
articles.description: The retailer is tapping the card network and U.S. Bank for two new credit cards aimed at small business owners.

articles.title: Visa pushes subscription management tool
articles.description: The card network is pitching a tool it says will make it easier to manage and cancel subscriptions in a single digital hub.

articles.title: Visa-DOJ discovery dispute goes to judge
articles.description: Visa is asking a federal court to intervene in a conflict over the company’s discovery request for U.S. Treasury documents.

articles.title: Visa’s stablecoin ally falls for Mastercard
articles.description: Stablecoin startup BVNK crowed about an investment from the largest U.S. card network last year, but now it’s being acquired by a rival.

articles.title: How Amex exploits new AI tools
articles.description: The bank has identified “hundreds” of use cases for artificial intelligence, its CEO says.

articles.title: Transforming the payment experience: How consumers are steering payment innovation
articles.description: Merchants can create enhanced shopping experiences that exceed evolving consumer expectations.
